summary: Full backend and frontend testing of Legal Intelligence System MVP. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI fully functional - landing page, case submission, analysis dashboard, judge profiles, and case history all working correctly. LLM Council analysis completes successfully with real Claude Sonnet API calls.

issues: Bias gauge bar appears mostly as gradient without clear pointer visualization - cosmetic only, not functional

critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 101: Re-analysis check only prevents re-running if status is 'analyzing', but if status is 'complete' it deletes and re-runs - this is intentional per the comment but may cause confusion for users who visit the analysis page again

AnalysisDashboard.jsx: startPolling() is called inside init() but startPolling uses a closure that may not have the latest state. However, the polling uses setAnalysis which should work correctly with React state updates.
No pagination on /api/cases - hardcoded limit of 100 may be insufficient at scale
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 6 judges and 15 laws on startup. Test case 8349fb86-468a-45b6-8f9a-6cf59c14a608 exists with complete analysis.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core flows working. Backend: 13/13 tests passing. Frontend: Landing page hero/features/council sections all visible. Navbar navigation to /submit, /judges, /history all work. Case submission form submits and redirects to /analysis/:caseId. Analysis dashboard shows 4 council cards (prosecution, defense, legal_scholar, bias_detector) + chief justice card. LLM analysis completes in ~60s with real AI responses. Judge profiles page shows 6 judges with modal+bias chart. Case history page shows submitted cases with status.
